title: 2018 AMC 12B Problems/Problem 9
solution: Recall that the sum of the first $100$ positive integers is $\sum^{100}_{k=1} k = \frac{101\cdot100}{2}=5050.$ It follows that \begin{align*} \sum^{100}_{i=1} \sum^{100}_{j=1} (i+j) &= \sum^{100}_{i=1} \sum^{100}_{j=1}i + \sum^{100}_{i=1} \sum^{100}_{j=1}j \\ &= \sum^{100}_{i=1} (100i) + 100 \sum^{100}_{j=1}j \\ &= 100 \sum^{100}_{i=1}i + 100 \sum^{100}_{j=1}j \\ &= 100\cdot5050 + 100\cdot5050 \\ &= \boxed{\textbf{(E) }1{,}010{,}000}. \end{align*} ~MRENTHUSIASM
